2026-03-25T15:12:36.7283058Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-25T15:11:23.897550048Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57467,\"title\":\"[BUG] Handle user concent using the Azure.AI.Projects SDK\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nI have an application calling the AI Agent in the new Foundry using Azure.AI.Projects. The agent has one tool registered using `OAuth Identity Passthrough` Authentication.\\n\\nAs long as the agent is not using any tools, it works, but when it does, I get an empty response.\\n\\nIf I make the Agent call using my own DefaultAzureCredential, it works if I have tested the agent from the portal and given concent, while if I use a service principle always fails. \\n\\nI use the `CreateResponseStreamingAsync`:\\n```csharp\\ncurrentResponseId = await StreamRoundAsync(\\n    responseClient.CreateResponseStreamingAsync(followUpOptions, cancellationToken),\\n    onToken, onToolCall, cancellationToken,\\n    pendingApprovals);\\n\\n...\\n\\nprivate async Task StreamRoundAsync(\\n    IAsyncEnumerable stream,\\n    Func onToken,\\n    Func? onToolCall,\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken,\\n    List pendingApprovals)\\n{\\n    string? responseId = null;\\n    await foreach (var update in stream.WithCancellation(cancellationToken))\\n    {\\n        _logger.LogInformation(\\u0026#34;Agent update: {Type}\\u0026#34;, update.GetType().Name);\\n\\n        if (update is StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate createdUpdate)\\n        {\\n            responseId = createdUpdate.Response.Id;\\n        }\\n        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputTextDeltaUpdate textDelta \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp;\\n                    !string.IsNullOrEmpty(textDelta.Delta))\\n        {\\n            await onToken(textDelta.Delta);\\n        }\\n        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate itemDone \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp;\\n                    itemDone.Item is McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem approvalRequest)\\n        {\\n            var requestItem = itemDone.Item as McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem;\\n            var toolName = requestItem?.ToolName ?? \\u0026#34;UnknownTool\\u0026#34;;\\n            _logger.LogWarning(\\u0026#34;MCP approval request on server: {Server} {ToolName}\\u0026#34;, approvalRequest.ServerLabel, toolName);\\n\\n            if (onToolCall != null)\\n                await onToolCall(approvalRequest.ServerLabel ?? string.Empty);\\n            pendingApprovals.Add(approvalRequest);\\n        }\\n                \\n    }\\n    return responseId;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Scenario 1: Using my own credentials for the first time or using as service priciple\\nThe agent returns an empty response\\nStreamingResponseUpdate events:\\n```bash\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsFailedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: UnknownResponseStreamEvent\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate\\n```\\n## Scenario 2: Using my own credentials AFTER concent in portal\\nThe agent returns an empty response\\nStreamingResponseUpdate events:\\n```bash\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsCompletedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      MCP approval request on server: IntuneMcpTool list_devices\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate\\n      Auto-approving MCP tool call on: IntuneMcpTool\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDeltaUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallCompletedUpdate\\n```\\n\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nI can handle approval of using tools, but not **user concent**. I would like there to be an event where I can hamdle user concent.\\n\\nPS. The fact that it \\u0026#34;works\\u0026#34; using my own account proviced I\\u0026#39;ve given concent in the portal is strange to me., and only works until the agent hibernates.\\n\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nNo event is raised allowing me to accept and give concent\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nSee code above\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n```bash\\n.NET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.200\\n Commit:            40cd698e5c\\n Workload version:  10.0.200-manifests.b7278506\\n MSBuild version:   18.3.0-release-26119-122+40cd698e5\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26200\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-arm64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.200\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. Run \\u0026#34;dotnet workload restore\\u0026#34; to install a workload set.\\n\\nHost:\\n  Version:      10.0.4\\n  Architecture: arm64\\n  Commit:       80d3e14f5e\\n\\n.NET SDKs installed:\\n  9.0.304 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  10.0.100 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n  10.0.200 [C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk]\\n```\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57467\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"wmmihaa\",\"id\":3214367,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/wmmihaa\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3214367?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-25T15:09:11Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-25T15:09:11Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

The agent has one tool registered using `OAuth Identity Passthrough` Authentication.\\n\\nAs long as the agent is not using any tools, it works, but when it does, I get an empty response.\\n\\nIf I make the Agent call using my own DefaultAzureCredential, it works if I have tested the agent from the portal and given concent, while if I use a service principle always fails. \\n\\nI use the `CreateResponseStreamingAsync`:\\n```csharp\\ncurrentResponseId = await StreamRoundAsync(\\n    responseClient.CreateResponseStreamingAsync(followUpOptions, cancellationToken),\\n    onToken, onToolCall, cancellationToken,\\n    pendingApprovals);\\n\\n...\\n\\nprivate async Task\\u003cstring?\\u003e StreamRoundAsync(\\n    IAsyncEnumerable\\u003cStreamingResponseUpdate\\u003e stream,\\n    Func\\u003cstring, Task\\u003e onToken,\\n    Func\\u003cstring, Task\\u003e? onToolCall,\\n    CancellationToken cancellationToken,\\n    List\\u003cMcpToolCallApprovalRequestItem\\u003e pendingApprovals)\\n{\\n    string? responseId = null;\\n    await foreach (var update in stream.WithCancellation(cancellationToken))\\n    {\\n        _logger.LogInformation(\\\"Agent update: {Type}\\\", update.GetType().Name);\\n\\n        if (update is StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate createdUpdate)\\n        {\\n            responseId = createdUpdate.Response.Id;\\n        }\\n        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputTextDeltaUpdate textDelta \\u0026\\u0026\\n                    !string.IsNullOrEmpty(textDelta.Delta))\\n        {\\n            await onToken(textDelta.Delta);\\n        }\\n        else if (update is StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate itemDone \\u0026\\u0026\\n                    itemDone.Item is McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem approvalRequest)\\n        {\\n            var requestItem = itemDone.Item as McpToolCallApprovalRequestItem;\\n            var toolName = requestItem?.ToolName ?? \\\"UnknownTool\\\";\\n            _logger.LogWarning(\\\"MCP approval request on server: {Server} {ToolName}\\\", approvalRequest.ServerLabel, toolName);\\n\\n            if (onToolCall != null)\\n                await onToolCall(approvalRequest.ServerLabel ?? string.Empty);\\n            pendingApprovals.Add(approvalRequest);\\n        }\\n                \\n    }\\n    return responseId;\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Scenario 1: Using my own credentials for the first time or using as service priciple\\nThe agent returns an empty response\\nStreamingResponseUpdate events:\\n```bash\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsFailedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: UnknownResponseStreamEvent\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate\\n```\\n## Scenario 2: Using my own credentials AFTER concent in portal\\nThe agent returns an empty response\\nStreamingResponseUpdate events:\\n```bash\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpListToolsCompletedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemDoneUpdate\\n      MCP approval request on server: IntuneMcpTool list_devices\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCompletedUpdate\\n      Auto-approving MCP tool call on: IntuneMcpTool\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseCreatedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseOutputItemAddedUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallInProgressUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDeltaUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallArgumentsDoneUpdate\\n      Agent update: StreamingResponseMcpCallCompletedUpdate\\n```\\n\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nI can handle approval of using tools, but not **user concent**. I would like there to be an event where I can hamdle user concent.\\n\\nPS. The fact that it \\\"works\\\" using my own account proviced I've given concent in the portal is strange to me., and only works until the agent hibernates.\\n\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nNo event is raised allowing me to accept and give concent\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nSee code above\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n```bash\\n.NET SDK:\\n Version:           10.0.200\\n Commit:            40cd698e5c\\n Workload version:  10.0.200-manifests.b7278506\\n MSBuild version:   18.3.0-release-26119-122+40cd698e5\\n\\nRuntime Environment:\\n OS Name:     Windows\\n OS Version:  10.0.26200\\n OS Platform: Windows\\n RID:         win-arm64\\n Base Path:   C:\\\\Program Files\\\\dotnet\\\\sdk\\\\10.0.200\\\\\\n\\n.NET workloads installed:\\nThere are no installed workloads to display.\\nConfigured to use workload sets when installing new manifests.\\nNo workload sets are installed. Foundry portal, and I have an application using `Azure.AI.Projects` to access the agent.\\n\\nIf I use `DefaultAzureCredential()`, it all works if I use `ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, behavior\\n\\nIf the Service Principal does not have required privileges, I would expect some exception\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe response comes back as an empty string\\n```csharp\\nResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(\\\"Tell me a joke\\\");\\nvar response = response.GetOutputText(); // response is empty\\n```\\n\\n### Reproduction Create C# console app and add the following nuget packages:\\n* Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1\\n* Azure.AI.Projects 2.0.0-beta.2\\n* Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI 2.0.0-beta.1\\n* Azure.Identity 1.19.0\\n* OpenAI 2.9.1\\n5. \\\"...\\\";\\nconst string agentName = \\\"...\\\";\\nconst string tenantId = \\\"...\\\";\\nconst string clientId = \\\"...\\\";\\nconst string clientSecret = \\\"...\\\";\\n\\n// This works\\n//AIProjectClient projectClient = new(endpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint), tokenProvider: new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n// This fails\\nvar credential = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);\\nAIProjectClient projectClient = new AIProjectClient(endpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint), tokenProvider: credential);\\n\\nvar responseClient = projectClient.OpenAI.GetProjectResponsesClientForAgent(agentName);\\n\\nConsole.WriteLine(\\\"Ask me something\\\");\\nvar prompt = Console.ReadLine();\\nwhile (prompt != null)\\n{\\n    // Use the agent to generate a response\\n    ResponseResult response = responseClient.CreateResponse(prompt);\\n\\n    Console.WriteLine($\\\"OUTPUT: {response.GetOutputText()}\\\");\\n
